text:
The Victorian government has confirmed new details about the welcome home parade which will be held in Melbourne for the state's Olympic and Paralympic athletes.

New details have emerged about the welcome home parade which will be held for Victoria's Olympians and Paralympians in Melbourne next week.

The Victorian government confirmed on Wednesday the celebration will take place at Melbourne Park Oval on Olympic Boulevard from 3.30pm on September 14.

The free event will provide Victorians with "the chance to meet and interact with Olympic and Paralympic athletes" who competed in Paris this year.

"Victoria's Olympic and Paralympic athletes have done us proud in Paris," Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan said in a statement.

"This is our chance to give them the welcome home they deserve and celebrate their success.

"Like so many families, ours couldn't get enough of the games. And I hope coming along to meet some of our stars will inspire the next generation of athletes."

The government said the event will also feature "a range of entertainment", including "live sport activations, DJ performances (and) food trucks".

The event will be held six days after the completion of the Paralympics. The Olympics concluded on August 11.

Ms Allan in Marchrevealed her governmenthad provided a financial contribution to support Australians competing in the Paris games.

The $1.5 million was shared equally between Olympic and Paralympic athletes.

The news came amid concerns the government had been giving athletes the "run-around" after being asked for funding last October and again last December.

Shadow tourism, sport and events minister Sam Groth blasted the government for taking so long to confirm the funding, after previously axing the 2026 Commonwealth Games planned for regional Victoria.

"It's a disgrace that Labor could find $600 million to cancel the Commonwealth games, but gave Olympic and Paralympic athletes the run-around for months over just $1.5 million," Mr Groth, a former Olympian himself, said in March.

New South Wales will hold a welcome home event in Tumbalong Park, Darling Harbour, on September 13 from 12pm.

In Queensland, the state's welcome home celebration will take place at the Cultural Forecourt in Brisbane's South Bank Parklands on September 14 from 9am to 12pm.
